title: WIP - Remove 'container-' from registries.conf man links
body:
There's a bug and containers-registries.conf(5) is not
available atm.  Reset it to registires.conf(5) until
that is rectified.

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

`containers-registries.conf` is the correct name. It is purely a bug in the Fedora (and potentially RHEL) packages.

Performing this change will cause regressions for other distributions which I object.
Didn't we discuss this issue already over at #2776?
I thought I saw a note from @rhatdan to make the change.  Let's leave this up until he checks in.

> I thought I saw a note from @rhatdan to make the change. Let's leave this up until he checks in.

I didn't see it but to summarize my points:
- The manpages in question come from containers/image: https://github.com/containers/image/tree/master/docs
- The `containers-` prefix is there since August 2018 and affects more manpages than registries.conf
- References to those manpages have been updated across our projects (including CRI-O)

This PR reverts one reference of many manpages in one of many projects and will cause regressions for other distros. Installing the latest manpages in Fedora is my preferred solution.

The whole idea of this PR was because containers-registries.conf did not exist.  I am not checking on my Fedora 29 system and I see the `man containers-registries.conf` works, as well as `man registries.conf`
Therefore we should go back to the original bug and figure out if the distribution that is reporting the issue on requires updated package.
I have fixed the skopeo package in Fedora Master to do the right thing, and ship containers-registries.conf.   The next time we ship Skopeo this issue should be fixed.
Thanks, @rhatdan! Did you check if other manpages from containers/image (see https://github.com/containers/image/tree/master/docs) need to be renamed in the package as well?
